scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced a coordination-only handoff summary with current owner, receiving owner, authority basis, source records, due date, and closure confirmation without taking the program decision.
The incomplete upstream referral was blocked with missing signed referral, notice history, and audit-link prerequisites rather than marked ready for transfer.
Conflicting ownership and approval authority triggered jurisdiction and authority escalation instead of an informal owner selection.
The specialist refused substantive exception approval and routed the evidence packet to the documented approving authority.
Eligibility, notice, and appeal-timing risk triggered due-process escalation with records preserved and no eligibility interpretation.
The missing official repository record and correspondence log blocked closure and routed the issue to the records owner with audit-trail gaps named.
The enforcement or adjudication request was refused and routed to program adjudication, enforcement, or legal authority owners.
The incomplete GT&C, funding authority, acquisition determination, and requesting-agency approval package was escalated without obligating funds or approving acquisition action.
The specialist stopped on missing jurisdiction, policy, statute, systems, owner matrix, and public-records rules before routing.
Conflicting program manual, MOU, and records-policy sources were escalated with source hierarchy gaps instead of confidently synthesized.
Tenant-specific privacy, dual-confirmation, and regional-routing constraints were applied while preserving the public baseline and retrieval assumptions.
Stale receiving-owner acceptance produced a blocker escalation with owner state, SLA risk, evidence links, and closure criteria without closing the transfer.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and the government-and-public-sector abilities required by the queue row.
The package is validated as a spec pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
